_note: Repaired 2026-05-24 after FUSE truncation during yae-apothecary-card-stuck-v0.5 edit. Item set unchanged from pre-truncation state.

items.prompt: HBH Numbers tab in docs/GDD.html hand-encodes HP, cost, defense, worker counts, footprint for every v1 building. The .gd files in source/buildings/ are the actual truth. Today caught 3 rows drifted (Manor, House, Research Lab). Structural fix: add a build-time pre-processor to publish-gdd.ps1 that reads source/buildings/*.gd, extracts the @export and const values, and rewrites the Numbers tab rows. Same pattern applies to the Walls + Towers table and Units + Enemies stat tables.

The cross-project-status artifact hardcodes the previous session VM mount root. Each Cowork session gets a new mount root, so the live-refresh git + queue + audit panels silently fail. Fix: discover the mount root dynamically at refresh time via ls -d /sessions/*/mnt/YesAndEverything 2>/dev/null | head -1, then substring-extract the session segment, then build all per-repo paths from that base. Apply to all five hardcoded path references in the artifact JS.

GDD section 9 Tier 4 Wonders Effect column drifts from shipped code on every row and needs author judgement on which design wins. Cornucopia: GDD says +5.0 Provisions/s + cap halo, code is 4.0 Provisions/s no cap halo. Perpetual Engine: GDD says +50 Power capacity, code is 5.0 Power/s + 60 Coal seed. Pyre: GDD says noise emitter, 5000 HP, emergency wave; code is -6/min Terror credit, 1200 HP. Sanctum: GDD says -20/min Terror, code is +1 HP/s/unit map-wide heal. Decision needed: amend section 9 to match code, or rewrite code to match section 9.
X:\YesAndApothecary\PROJECT_SPEC.md uses section number `## 11.` twice: line 264 (Data storage decision) and line 276 (Cross-references). Renumber the second one to `## 12. Cross-references`.

PROJECT_SPEC.md §4.1 herb schema lists only 7 fields. v0.3 added 4 back-label fields to every herb in data/herbs.json. Extend the schema example to include: descFull (<=260), historicUses (<=180), nutrition (<=140), pairings (<=100).

PROJECT_SPEC.md §4.7 template descriptor schema is missing v0.3 keys: backZones, descFullMaxChars, historicMaxChars, nutritionMaxChars, pairingsMaxChars. Also append back-side ITEM_RENDERERS keys (back-name, back-latin, back-divider, back-desc-full, back-historic-section, back-bottom-row) to the item-keys paragraph.

data/aliases.json carries `"st. johns wort": "st johns wort"` which is dead weight: src/util/lookup.js strips dots before alias lookup, and PROJECT_SPEC §4.2 explicitly states no need to alias both dot and non-dot variants. Remove the line.

PROJECT_SPEC.md section 9 roadmap carries duplicate milestone headers after M5: a second M3 Export and M4 Themes. These conflict with the real M3 (Back label) and M4 (Multi-culture data). Nick's call: delete entirely, demote to Backlog, or renumber into M6/M7.

X:\YesAndApothecary\push.ps1 has commit + mirror + Discord but no file-integrity check before the deploy mirror runs. Add a Test-ApothecaryIntegrity function. Checks: index.html ends with </html>, src/main.js parses (node --check), no conflict markers in tracked files, PROJECT_SPEC.md Version pill matches Get-VersionFromSpec. Also: WEBHOOK_URL still reads PASTE_WEBHOOK_URL_HERE -- needs Nick to set the actual Discord webhook URL for #apothecary-dev-log.

source/ui/gameplay/gameplay.gd REPAIR_ALL_PRIORITY dict contains key 'Storage Shed': 3. No building with building_name 'Storage Shed' exists in source/buildings/. storehouse.gd uses 'Warehouse'; warehouse.gd uses 'Substation'. Dead code that silently never matches. Decision: rename key to 'Warehouse', remove it, or add the intended building. Not auto-safe -- affects gameplay repair priority ordering.

DESIGN.md §7.3 says 'Each save records to a preference_log table for auditability.' No preference_log table exists in any migration. No code writes to it. Options: (a) add migration 0008 + write logic, (b) remove the claim from §7.3, (c) emit audit_log events from the preferences route on every change -- reuses the existing append-only audit_log without a new table. Nick's call.

X:\YesAndEverything\projects\here-there-be-hordes\gdd.html is the public-preview GDD page (lighter weight than the password-gated hordes/ mirror). Highest version mentioned in the file is v0.26.16; actual HBH is past v0.61.x. The page is ~35 minor versions behind. Two viable paths: (A) republish the preview by extracting a sanitized subset of the current GDD, or (B) drop the standalone preview and replace it with a link/iframe to the live hordes/ gate. Needs Nick's call on which path before action.

GDD section 3 'Live sprite tuning reference' heading reads '(v0.61.2)' but GDD pill is v0.65.0. The v0.61.2 pass was comprehensive and values appear current, but the stale stamp erodes trust. After htbh-uncommitted-v0-65-0-release ships: find the heading at docs/GDD.html line ~2453 and update the stamp from '(v0.61.2)' to the current GDD version. Bump GDD one PATCH and add changelog entry. Doc-only, auto-safe.

items.prompt: PROJECT_SPEC.md §5 state shape is missing 4 fields added since v0.4: notesSplit (v0.6, boolean, back-label display mode), placement (v0.4, object, per-item visibility), parchmentTexture (v0.8.2, string, slot id from data/textures.js), shopColor (v0.8.2, string hex, shop-name color override). Also: icon field exists in defaultState() with no spec mention -- annotate as vestigial (no active consumer since v0.7.1 retired the icon registry). PROJECT_SPEC.md file layout table is missing four entries added since v0.8.0. Add rows for: data/textures.js (25 parchment slot ids, imported in src/main.js), data/rune-meanings.json (flat char->meaning map, loaded in src/main.js line 62), data/category-defaults.js (botanical category defaults, referenced by tombstoned categories.js), data/symbols/ directory (10 PNG files: hollow+solid variants of awen, celtic-cross, celtic-knot, shield-knot, solar-wheel; primary symbol source, renderer falls back to inline SVG in symbols.js). File: X:\YesAndApothecary\PROJECT_SPEC.md.

PROJECT_SPEC.md §4.4 Symbol schema documents only the SYMBOLS and SYMBOL_LABELS JS registry. The data/symbols/ PNG directory (10 files, added v0.8.0) and the two-layer rendering contract are not mentioned. Extend §4.4 to: (1) document data/symbols/<id>-(hollow|solid).png as the primary symbol source, (2) note that render.js layers the PNG img over the inline SVG fallback, (3) note onerror on the img removes it so the SVG shows through if the PNG is missing. File: X:\YesAndApothecary\PROJECT_SPEC.md.

PROJECT_SPEC.md §10 Backlog still lists 'Populate data/ingredients/ with PNG art for the 80 herb entries (sourcing job).' as open. data/ingredients/ contains 200 PNGs covering all 199 current herb entries. Mark this item done: prefix with '[DONE v0.8.x]' and update the entry count reference from 80 to 199. File: X:\YesAndApothecary\PROJECT_SPEC.md.

data/categories.js opens with '// REMOVED v0.8.2: locked decision, no SVG in plant slot.' and is empty of functional code. Confirmed not imported by anything in src/. Delete the file: rm X:\YesAndApothecary\data\categories.js, then commit via scripts/release.ps1 -Message 'remove tombstoned categories.js stub' -Bump none. Shell op -- not auto-safe.

items.prompt: In docs/GDD.html backlog JS array, find S-39 'Centralize TILE_W / TILE_H / FRAMES_PER_ROW' entry. Update the notes field: the item is now partially done. constants.gd autoload has TILE_W=64 / TILE_H=32 / FRAMES_PER_ROW=15 as canonical (shipped v0.26.94 partial). The old claim 'TILE_W=128/TILE_H=64' is pre-v0.61.0 and wrong. The old claim 'constants.gd is currently nearly empty' is wrong. Rewrite notes to say: 'PARTIAL v0.26.94. constants.gd autoload is the canonical source (TILE_W=64, TILE_H=32, FRAMES_PER_ROW=15). Five files still mirror these as local consts for compile-time use (iso_grid, building, enemy_base, unit, tree_sprite) -- mirrors are intentional due to GDScript const-initializer limitation, documented at each site. DIRECTION_ROWS=8 and shared flags (FADED_ALPHA etc.) not yet centralized. Full centralization blocked by GDScript compile-time const limitation.' File: X:\HereBeHordes\docs\GDD.html.

In docs/GDD.html Progress tab JS array, find the entry with id 'code-reinforced-wall'. Currently s: 'progress', n: 'v0.40.0 reinstated; building exists, art pending'. Update to s: 'done' and update n to: 'v0.40.0 reinstated; code wired, art pending'. The code is complete (reinforced_wall.gd + .tscn exist with full cost/HP/defense values). Art-pending is a separate art backlog item. File: X:\HereBeHordes\docs\GDD.html.

The scheduled audit-htbh-daily task fires in a Cowork session whose mount set is YaE + Scheduler + YaC. X:\HereThereBeHordes is unreachable, so the HBH source-side walk (locked decisions, sprite tuning table, roadmap 'done' vs. source/*.gd, project.godot version pill) cannot run. Two viable fixes: (A) add X:\HereThereBeHordes to the scheduled-task session mount, or (B) pre-step that git-clones a shallow read-only copy into outputs/ before the audit runs. Option A is cleaner.

CANONICAL_AUDIT-2026-05-23.md ended with a 'Queue-these' block listing three items; none appear in .work-queue.json. Either the work-queue-runner is not parsing audit footers, or the convention has always required the audit skill itself to invoke 'work-queue-runner add' for each item. Pick one: (A) teach work-queue-runner to scan recent docs/CANONICAL_AUDIT-*.md and HANDLER_AUDIT-*.md for Queue-these blocks and pull them in on next drain, or (B) update project-canonical-audit and handler-audit skill SKILL.md to call work-queue-runner add explicitly before exit and stop emitting the markdown footer.

project.godot config/version is v0.74.38, four versions behind GDD pill v0.74.42. Release script appears to only bump on MINOR. Add PATCH bump to scripts/release.ps1 so the engine version pill stays synced with the GDD pill.

The scheduled HBH canonical-doc audit task has run three days in a row (2026-05-23, -24, -25) without X:\HereThereBeHordes mounted in the Cowork session. Source-side walk has been skipped each time. Fix options: (a) add HBH to the mount set for the audit-htbh-daily scheduled task, or (b) pre-pull source/ and docs/GDD.html via git into a side workspace the audit can reach (e.g. X:\YesAndEverything\.audit-mirror\htbh\). The audit can validate the YaE mirror without HBH access but cannot check locked decisions, roadmap done claims, sprite tuning, or project.godot version pill without source.

DESIGN.md §15: in the `users` column list, add `email_lower (unique, NOT NULL)` and remove the `unique` qualifier from `email` (leave it as `NOT NULL`). Matches migrations/0002_auth.sql, which puts the unique index on email_lower not email. Resolves 2026-05-24 audit MEDIUM-5 + LOW-11.

DESIGN.md §15 `shifts` row: add `generation_seed` (INTEGER, nullable) to the column list. Backs the §8.6 determinism claim. Defined in migrations/0004_schedule.sql line ~16. Resolves 2026-05-24 audit LOW-6.

DESIGN.md §17 Page/Route Map: remove the `/auth/callback` line (Supabase SDK handles the magic-link return inline inside AuthProvider, no React route) and remove the `/admin` Admin overview line (App.tsx has no such route; only /admin/departments, /admin/users, /admin/audit, /admin/settings exist). Resolves 2026-05-24 audit LOW-7 + LOW-8.

DESIGN.md §19 repo-layout tree: replace the single `.github/workflows/deploy.yml # on push to main: deploy worker + pages` line with the actual two files: `ci.yml # lint/typecheck/test on PR` and `deploy-pages.yml # deploy SPA + worker on push to main`. Resolves 2026-05-24 audit LOW-9.

Scheduler CLAUDE.md line 17 (M2 bullet) currently says `M2 — Auth & users. Login, logout, force-password-change, admin-only user creation.` Rewrite to match DESIGN §21 M2: `M2 — Auth & users. Magic-link sign-in via Supabase, users table, first-login auto-provision into pending state, first-admin bootstrap from env.` Auth has been magic-link since v0.1.0; force-password-change never existed. Resolves 2026-05-24 audit LOW-10.

DESIGN.md §20 opening sentence currently says `The Scheduler stores no PII beyond name, optional email, hashed password, and work-preference data.` Rewrite to `The Scheduler stores no PII beyond name, email, Supabase user id, and work-preference data.` Email is required (§5.1); no password is ever stored (identity delegated to Supabase). Resolves 2026-05-24 audit LOW-12.
